pre_registered_hypotheses.H1_weakens: adv_o2 < +5 && adv_o3 < +5  → Law 64 v5 retraction candidate
pre_registered_hypotheses.H3_degraded: o2_acc < o1_acc || o3_acc < o1_acc → robust by accident

narrative: CA(5) advantage GROWS from +160 (o1) to +169 (o3) per thousand. Higher-order Markov DOES NOT close the gap; it slightly degrades from order-1 due to per-cell context sparsity (50 train pairs split across 4 contexts in o2 and 8 in o3). Law 64 v4 is robust to n-gram baseline choice — the structural advantage of CA(5) is not an artifact of picking the weakest surrogate.

raw_91_honesty.C1_falsifiable: YES — pre-registered numeric thresholds (5 and 10 per thousand) frozen in tool header before run
raw_91_honesty.C2_replicable: YES — seed=20260428 deterministic; rerun reproduces exact 1000/862/856/855
raw_91_honesty.C3_no_p_hacking: YES — verdict mapping cascade frozen in header (H3 then H2 override then H1 override)
raw_91_honesty.C4_data_caveats: 10x10 / 32% / random init (NOT glider). Higher-order sparsity at N_TRAIN=50 means o2/o3 are pessimistically estimated; with N_TRAIN→∞ o2/o3 should asymptote at o1 or slightly above (still well below CA(5)).
raw_91_honesty.C5_negative_result_published: N/A this run was POSITIVE for v4; emission is unconditional regardless of outcome

next_open_questions: Q1 still open: 80x80 N=1500 aperiodic R-pentomino test (task #24)
Sub-Q for Q2: rerun T8l with N_STEPS=600 (300 train) to confirm H3 sparsity caveat — does o2/o3 then beat o1 (still trailing CA(5))?
